title: DECIDE: first supported agent + native-vs-reconstructed shim strategy
description: The PRD's open question that bounds everything downstream. Pick ONE coding agent to support first (e.g. SWE-agent, OpenHands, or a Claude/OTel harness) and decide whether it emits the 8-span schema NATIVELY (clean, high-fidelity, agent must cooperate) or whether spans are RECONSTRUCTED from heterogeneous logs (broader support, lower fidelity; reconstructed spans are tagged and excluded from R7/R8 per R-silent). Also pick the benchmark: FixedBench (abstention/R7), SWE-Explore (localization/retrieval), or both. This choice determines units aoa.shim, aoa.bench, and the eval-run command.
design: SUPERSEDED after reuse pass (2026-06-19): Wave 0.5 builds ON codeprobe (~/projects/codeprobe), NOT a standalone SWE-agent+SWE-bench stack. codeprobe already provides: contamination-free task mining from repo history (solves R0b better than SWE-bench, which models may have memorized), agent orchestration (Claude Code/Copilot/Codex), baseline-vs-config experiments + anti-Goodhart bias detection (= the R0 comparison shape), dual verification, and consensus/AST oracle ground truth. AOA adds ONLY its wedge on top: the 8-span trace + 4 process/locality metrics + context-budget gate + R0' falsify hardening. Primary agent = Claude Code (codeprobe runs it with stream-json --verbose). Benchmark = codeprobe mined tasks. VERIFIED: codeprobe retains the full ordered tool-call transcript per trial (CompletedTask.agent_stdout via adapters/claude.py:393).
close_reason: Decided: SWE-agent + native spans + SWE-bench Verified. Recorded in design field.

title: Code-structure best-practices AUDIT family (aoa-audit structure checks, born Advisory/Tier3)
description: Add a code-structure best-practices audit family to crates/aoa-audit: a new structure.rs module producing MEASURED, MECHANICAL PunchItems (grounded counts/existence, NO semantic quality judgments, NO absolute magic thresholds), wired into audit() before rank(). Every check is born Tier3/Advisory per the R9c construct-validity discipline (aoa-gap/src/construct.rs) — structure measures do NOT become gating 'best-practices' until external-outcome correlation promotes them (deferred to aoa-mnz.3). This is the foundational measurable for R0's baseline-vs-migrated repo-delta arm: it lets 'how much better-organized is the migrated checkout' be a grounded number.
design: Insertion point: audit.rs:77 items.extend(structure_items(repo)?). PunchItem/MeasuredCost/Tier model from punch.rs+tier.rs. Mirror existing checks' discipline (context-budget=measured-vs-ceiling, mutation-surface=count, planes=existence). Honest line-scan best-effort allowed (precedent: aoa-scip-graph/src/best_effort.rs).
acceptance_criteria: 1) New crates/aoa-audit/src/structure.rs with >=2 mechanical, grounded structure checks each emitting PunchItem{measured_cost, tier=Tier3} only on a measured finding. 2) Wired into aoa_audit::audit() so 'aoa audit --json' surfaces structure items alongside planes. 3) Checks are self-calibrating or existence-based (no hardcoded absolute magic numbers presented as best-practice truth); language-specific checks degrade honestly on non-matching repos. 4) Born Advisory/Tier3 — no fabricated evidence tying structure to outcomes. 5) TDD: unit tests per check + an integration assertion that 'aoa audit' emits structure items on a fixture repo.
notes:
PLAN: GOAL — 'aoa audit' measures code-structure best-practice adherence as grounded, MEASURED numbers (born Tier3/Advisory), giving R0 a baseline-vs-migrated repo-delta measurable. APPROACH: (1) NEW crates/aoa-audit/src/structure.rs with mechanical, grounded checks each returning Vec<PunchItem> only on a measured finding, MeasuredCost{value,unit}, tier=Tier3. (2) Wire via items.extend(structure_items(repo)?) at audit.rs:77 before rank(). (3) Initial checks (mechanical, no semantic judgment, no absolute magic-number-as-truth): (a) navigability-anchor existence — repo-root + per-top-level-package entry doc (README) present (existence, like planes); (b) module-size outliers — count source files exceeding k× the repo's OWN median source-file line count (relative/self-calibrating, k a documented config knob like mutation-surface depth, NOT an absolute 'best practice' magic number); (c) public-API doc coverage — best-effort line-scan count of pub items lacking a preceding doc line (Rust; honest degrade on non-Rust, precedent best_effort.rs). (4) All Advisory/Tier3 — promotion to gating deferred to aoa-mnz.3 (R9c external-outcome correlation). FILES: NEW structure.rs; audit.rs (wire + structure_items); lib.rs (export for test); Cargo.toml (walkdir if needed); NEW tests in structure.rs + tests/acceptance.rs; possibly a fixture repo under tests/. TESTS: per-check unit (finding vs no-finding, self-calibration, honest degrade); integration that 'aoa audit --json' emits structure items on a fixture. RISKS: magic-threshold construct-validity (mitigate: relative/self-calibrating + Tier3-only + defer gating); cross-language honesty (mitigate: degrade, don't fabricate); over-engineering the check set (mitigate: start with 2-3, framework extensible). OUT OF SCOPE: migration execution (aoa-mnz.2); R9c gating wiring (aoa-mnz.3); promoting any check above Tier3.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial — folded in): HIGH-1 DROP check (c) doc-coverage — presenting undocumented-pub-item COUNT as a punch-list cost asserts undocumented=deficient = unproven best-practice prior = Goodhart (runbook guardrail 3 + R9c construct.rs:221-226 + ZFC). Keep only MEASURED FACTS. HIGH-2 the k size-multiplier is a policy constant -> make it a documented overridable AuditConfig field w/ default (mirror GatingThresholds construct.rs:88-111), NOT a bare const. HIGH-3 wiring: AuditError (error.rs:8-24) has NO From<io::Error> (Io variant carries path) -> must manually .map_err(|source| AuditError::Io{path,source}) like best_effort.rs:69-77, raw '?' won't compile; signature must be structure_items(repo, cfg) (check b needs k from cfg); wire at audit.rs LINE 78 (77 is plane_items). MED honest-degrade not representable in PunchItem (no degraded state) — moot now that (c) dropped. MED source walk must replicate best_effort.rs symlink-skip + read-cap (bounded.rs is private to aoa-scip-graph; replicate minimal safety inline). AFFIRMED: Tier3 firewall real (report.rs:48-56 exit_code keys only on Tier1; nothing downstream reads PunchItem.tier as evidence; gap MetricMode is disjoint vocab); Tier3='asserted-but-unsupported' (tier.rs:3-8) fits. REVISED PLAN: exactly 2 checks, both measured facts, Tier3: (a) navigability-anchor existence (README absent per top-level package; title 'anchor absent' neutral, existence like planes.rs); (b) module-size outliers (count source files > cfg.size_outlier_k x repo's OWN median source-file line count; self-calibrating; k documented config default). Signature structure_items(repo,&cfg)->Result<Vec<PunchItem>,AuditError>; manual IO wrap; bounded walk (skip symlinks, cap bytes). RESIDUAL CAVEAT: even (a)/(b) add AOA-authored structural surfacing to the tool that verifies R0 — they survive ONLY because they're measured facts not opinions; keep titles observational, Tier3, gating deferred to aoa-mnz.3.
PROGRESS: Implemented per revised plan. NEW crates/aoa-audit/src/structure.rs with 2 measured-fact checks born Tier3: (a) navigability_anchor_item = count of package roots (repo root + immediate child dirs carrying a build manifest) lacking a README (existence, like planes); (b) module_size_outlier_item = count source files > size_outlier_k x repo's OWN median source-file line count (self-calibrating; abstains <5 files or zero median). Added AuditConfig.size_outlier_k (default 4.0, documented overridable). Wired items.extend(structure_items(repo, cfg.size_outlier_k)?) at audit.rs after plane_items. Manual AuditError::Io wrapping (no From<io::Error>); bounded source read (8MiB cap -> skip-not-fatal); recursive walk skips hidden + build-output dirs, never follows symlinks (mirrors best_effort.rs). TESTS: 9 unit (median odd/even, nav present/absent/manifest-children, size outlier/uniform/too-few/skip-build, symlink-not-followed) + integration audit_surfaces_structure_family_items in tests/acceptance.rs. GATES: full workspace test green; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
REVIEW: unified Phase-4 (code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer), 0 CRITICAL 0 HIGH, all APPROVE. Fixes applied in-bead: (1) MED [code+rust] count_lines now counts newline BYTES via read_to_end (was read_to_string) — a source-extension file with non-UTF-8/binary content is skipped/counted not fatal (previously InvalidData aborted the whole audit); regression test size_measure_counts_non_utf8_source_without_aborting. (2) MED [rust] median even-case -> u64::midpoint (overflow-safe). (3) MED [rust] added f64-precision bounding comment on the outlier cast (line counts capped by MAX_SOURCE_BYTES << 2^53). (4) LOW [all] added 3 branch tests: zero-median abstention, non-utf8 skip, hidden-dir skip. (5) LOW [code+security] documented has_manifest exists()-follows-symlinks (one-level fixed-name probe; reviewers confirmed not a vuln). Security: 0 issues — symlink traversal blocked by file_type(), 8MiB cap fencepost correct, zero-write (covered by audit_does_not_mutate_repo), no repo-derived strings in output. NOT changed (declined churn): has_readme alloc (O(1)); cross-crate bounded-walk dedup with best_effort.rs (rule-of-three, flagged not refactored). GATES: 12 unit + 8 acceptance + full workspace (32 suites) green; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
close_reason: DONE: code-structure best-practices audit family shipped in aoa-audit. Two measured-fact checks (navigability-anchor existence; self-calibrating module-size outliers), both born Tier-3/advisory (anti-Goodhart per R9c). AuditConfig.size_outlier_k (default 4.0). Reviewed by 3 agents (0 CRIT/HIGH); non-UTF-8 byte-count robustness, u64::midpoint, +3 branch tests applied. 12 unit + 8 acceptance + full workspace green; clippy -D warnings + fmt clean. Key decision: structure measures are FACTS not best-practices until external-outcome correlation promotes them (aoa-mnz.3). Merged on main at 09a1fd12bbd7fd028ca524815c52e95181ff502f.

title: Code-infrastructure migration EXECUTION (aoa-migrate crate + 'aoa migrate' plan/apply)
description: New crates/aoa-migrate + 'aoa migrate --plan/--apply' that consumes the aoa-audit structure punch-list and applies SAFE, REPRODUCIBLE, ORACLE-BLIND migrations to a repo checkout (archive-original + dry-run preview, precedent aoa-budget/src/fix.rs). Produces the baseline-vs-migrated checkout pair that is R0's repo-delta treatment. Mutations must be code-layer (compiler/tests/graph/navigation see them), never prompt/context-file injection.
acceptance_criteria: 1) aoa-migrate crate with a CodeFix trait + dry-run plan + apply. 2) 'aoa migrate' subcommand wired in cli.rs/main.rs. 3) Safe-by-default: --plan previews a diff, --apply writes with archive/rollback. 4) Migrations are mechanical+reproducible+oracle-blind. 5) Emits a baseline-vs-migrated pair usable as the R0 repo-delta arm.
notes:
PLAN: GOAL — new crates/aoa-migrate + `aoa migrate --plan/--apply` applying SAFE, REPRODUCIBLE, ORACLE-BLIND, CODE-LAYER migrations toward code-infra best-practices; produces baseline-vs-migrated checkout pair = R0 repo-delta treatment. APPROACH: (1) crates/aoa-migrate: CodeFix trait {id, describe, plan(repo)->Vec<PlannedChange>} read-only; PlannedChange{path,action=Create|Overwrite,new_content,old_content} for unified-diff preview; plan() aggregates+renders diff (writes nothing); apply() archives originals under .aoa/migrate/archive/, writes new content, records MigrationManifest JSON (per-change + archive + content hash) enabling rollback(). FIRST/ONLY shipped fix = NavigabilityAnchorFix: for each package root (repo root + immediate manifest-bearing child dirs) lacking a README, create a MECHANICAL README whose ENTIRE content is a pure function of the tree (pkg name from manifest/dirname + mechanically-sorted top-level entry index) -> zero authored prose => oracle-blind + reproducible + deterministic. MigrateError thiserror path-carrying Io (mirror AuditError, no From<io::Error>). (2) `aoa migrate` subcommand: cli.rs Command::Migrate+MigrateArgs{repo,--apply,--json}; main.rs dispatch; commands/migrate.rs + mod.rs run_migrate. Runs aoa_audit::audit() FIRST to surface grounding structure punch-list (consumes the audit) then plans fixes the audit confirmed. (3) Safe-by-default: --plan is default (preview diff, no writes); --apply writes w/ archive+manifest+rollback. (4) Construct-validity: mechanical/reproducible/oracle-blind; module-size-outlier NOT auto-fixable (splitting not mechanically safe) -> OUT OF SCOPE; migration applies documented spec AOA VERIFIES not DEFINES (anti-Goodhart). FILES: NEW crates/aoa-migrate/{Cargo.toml,src/lib.rs,error.rs,fix.rs,plan.rs,apply.rs,tests/acceptance.rs}; aoa/Cargo.toml(+dep); aoa/src/{cli.rs,main.rs,commands/mod.rs}; NEW aoa/src/commands/migrate.rs. TESTS: unit (find roots w/o README; deterministic content; no-op when README present; plan() read-only; apply() writes+manifest; rollback restores/removes; overwrite archives original) + acceptance (--plan writes nothing & previews; --apply creates anchor & re-audit no longer fires nav item; rollback restores) + assert_cmd CLI integration. RISKS: Goodhart (mitigate: spec-verify-not-define, mechanical content); prose leakage guardrail2 (mitigate: pure tree-derived content, zero descriptions); README borderline code-layer (mitigate: runbook lists navigable in-repo docs as claimed layer, README != prompt/context file); walk-dup w/ structure.rs (decide: minimal+rule-of-three-flag vs shared helper). OUT OF SCOPE: module-split auto-fix; R9c gating (aoa-mnz.3); codeprobe pair orchestration; formatter fix.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial — folded in): C1/C5 leak-channel — README content MUST be a pure function of tree structure only ({dir names, immediate entry names}); enforce as TESTED invariant (identical trees w/ different file BODIES -> byte-identical README; two runs -> identical). DECISION: title = directory name only (zero file-content read; strictly safer than parsing manifest pkg field the architect allowed). C2 harness-eligibility — generated README is code-layer ONLY for harnesses that do not auto-inject README into system context; document as an R0 eligibility precondition in migrate output + close note (else confounds repo-delta w/ harness-delta, runbook:51-52). C3/H2 Goodhart — fix conforms to a DOCUMENTED spec clause ("every package root carries a navigability anchor"), NOT "make audit stop firing"; re-audit after apply is a MECHANICAL post-write sanity check reported as verify-not-define (runbook guardrail 3); migration success = spec conformance, audit verifies. H1 (HIGH, accepted — expands into aoa-audit) — expose pub fn navigability_sites(repo)->Result<Vec<PathBuf>,AuditError> in aoa-audit/src/structure.rs; navigability_anchor_item uses it (count = sites.len()); aoa-migrate CONSUMES sites (no re-walk) -> "fix exactly what audit measured" structurally true, kills audit/apply divergence. H3 (accepted) — README is the only structure-punch-list-backed mechanical fix (module-split unsafe); architect rightly notes a compiler-verified dead-code/unused-import fix is a strictly better code-layer citizen but needs its own audit signal + cargo toolchain -> FILE as follow-up bead, names the 2nd CodeFix implementer (justifies the trait per M3 lonely_interface). M1/M2 (accepted) — MigrationManifest entries typed enum Created{path}|Modified{path,archive}; rollback dispatches on variant; PLAN-FIRST manifest persistence (record planned change before write); CREATE-EXCLUSIVE writes (create_new) — existing file at target is a hard error, NEVER overwrite (anti auto_correction); tests: target-exists error path + rollback-after-mid-apply leaves tree byte-identical. M4/AC5 — manifest records {fixes_applied, archive provenance}; apply mutates checkout in place, pre-apply state recoverable via archive = baseline, post-apply = migrated arm; full checkout-copy pair-orchestration is operator/runbook flow (aoa eval experiment), out of scope. M5 (accepted) — deterministic: sorted entries, skip hidden+build dirs (mirror SKIP_DIRS), no timestamps/abs paths. AFFIRMED right: --plan default+--apply, archive-then-write (fix.rs), exclude module-split, Tier-3 firewall intact (report.rs exit_code keys Tier1 only). REVISED FILES: + aoa-audit/src/structure.rs (extract navigability_sites) + aoa-audit/src/lib.rs (export); rest as planned.
PROGRESS: Implemented per revised plan. NEW crates/aoa-migrate: error.rs (MigrateError: Io path-carrying + Audit + TargetExists + Manifest), fix.rs (CodeFix trait{id,describe,plan}; PlannedChange{path,action,new_content,old_content}; ChangeAction Create|Overwrite; NavigabilityAnchorFix consumes aoa_audit::navigability_sites, content = pure tree function: dir-name title + sorted entry index, skips hidden+build dirs, zero file-body reads), plan.rs (MigrationPlan{changes,fix_ids}; build sorts by path for determinism; render_diff), apply.rs (MigrationManifest{fixes_applied,entries,eligibility_note}; ManifestEntry Created|Modified tagged enum; apply = plan-first manifest persist THEN create-exclusive writes / archive-before-overwrite; rollback reverse-order delete-created/restore-modified + cleanup; ELIGIBILITY_NOTE const documenting harness precondition), lib.rs exports. aoa-audit: extracted pub fn navigability_sites (shared walk; navigability_anchor_item now = sites.len()) + lib export. CLI: cli.rs Command::Migrate+MigrateArgs{repo,--apply,--rollback,--json}; main.rs dispatch; commands/migrate.rs (plan/apply/rollback, dual human+JSON register, runs audit for grounding + re-audit verify-not-define) + mod.rs. TESTS: aoa-migrate 12 unit + 3 acceptance (plan read-only/no-write; apply clears audit finding; rollback restores; idempotent+leak-blind anchor; overwrite archive+restore; partial-apply rollback to baseline; target-exists hard error; manifest round-trip) + aoa 3 CLI integration (dry-run no-write; apply->rollback round-trip; apply --json remaining=0). GATES: full workspace test green; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
REVIEW: unified Phase-4 (code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer), 0 CRITICAL. Three converged on the same root issue. FIXED in-bead: (1) HIGH [all 3] strip_prefix().unwrap_or() at apply.rs produced an out-of-repo archive path (PathBuf::join w/ absolute arg replaces base) -> a future Overwrite fix could write outside .aoa/. Now: new MigrateError::PathOutsideRepo, apply() strip_prefix is a HARD ERROR for every change, validated up front. Test apply_rejects_a_target_outside_the_repo. (2) HIGH [code] + M3 [rust] empty-plan / double apply clobbered the rollback manifest (fs::write truncates) -> data loss of the only rollback record. Now: apply() refuses when a manifest already exists (MigrateError::AlreadyApplied); CLI run_apply no-ops on empty plan (never writes a manifest). Tests apply_refuses_when_a_manifest_already_exists + CLI. (3) HIGH [rust H2] copy() misattributed IO errors to the target end -> undiagnosable rollback-restore failures. Now: MigrateError::Copy{from,to,source}. (4) LOW [security] hostile filenames (newline/backtick) could inject markdown into the agent-read anchor. Now: sanitize() strips backticks + neutralizes control chars in title + entries. Test anchor_content_neutralizes_hostile_filenames. DECLINED w/ rationale: rollback skip-on-missing-archive is correct for the sanctioned crash-before-write case (original intact); archive deletion is out of threat model (.aoa gitignored, writer already has repo access). ELIGIBILITY_NOTE String alloc / to_string_lossy shadowing / as-u64 cast = clippy-clean idiomatic, declined. FILED follow-up: nested-workspace navigability scope (code MEDIUM) -> aoa-mnz.5 (audit measurement change, not migrate). VERIFIED-RIGHT by reviewers: create-exclusive writes (O_EXCL, incl. dangling symlink), plan-first manifest persistence, archive-before-overwrite, symlink-walk never followed, navigability_sites==migration sites seam, anchor blind to file bodies, partial-apply rollback. GATES: aoa-migrate 15 unit + 3 acceptance, aoa 33 (incl. 3 migrate CLI); full workspace 35 suites green; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean.

title: AOA code-infrastructure best-practices: audit adherence + execute migrations (R0 repo-delta treatment capability)
description: The construct-valid R0 repo-delta treatment (per aoa-dhk.1 architect review) requires AOA to (a) AUDIT a codebase's adherence to code-infrastructure best-practices — structure, naming, organization, modularity, and navigable in-repo documentation the agent builds on — beyond today's enforcement-plane-only 'aoa audit' (CI/pre-commit/writable-mutation) and context-file 'aoa lint-context'; and (b) EXECUTE (where permitted) reproducible, mechanical migrations that nudge the infrastructure back toward those best-practices. This is the observability-guardrail layer (tool/skill/hook) that is AOA's claimed repo layer. It is the precondition for a construct-valid R0 campaign: without a tool-driven, oracle-blind, code-LAYER treatment, the migrated arm is hand-authored and R0 mostly returns honest 'inconclusive'. See docs/r0_runbook.md 'What counts as the AOA migration' for the three guardrails (code-layer not prompt-layer; authored blind to held-out oracle; treatment defined by a pre-registered spec AOA verifies, not defines — anti-Goodhart).
design: Architect (aoa-dhk.1 review) rejected the context-file framing: CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md are harness/prompt material -> confounds repo-delta with harness-delta. Code-layer treatment is the only framing that supports AOA's 'repo is the right layer' claim. codeprobe 'experiment add-config' only sets harness knobs, so the repo dimension must be realized by the repo STATE tasks run against. Today 'aoa audit' surfaces enforcement-plane gaps only (verified via aoa audit --json); the AOA locality metrics (edit_locality/mutation_depth) are structure-adjacent but not wired to real per-task repo data.
acceptance_criteria: 1) 'aoa audit' (or a new subcommand) measures code-structure best-practice adherence with grounded numbers, not just enforcement planes. 2) A migration capability applies mechanical, reproducible best-practice changes to a repo checkout, oracle-blind. 3) Treatment is code-layer (compiler/tests/graph/navigation see it), never prompt/context files. 4) 'migrated' is defined by a pre-registered spec AOA verifies; AOA's own pass-state does not define success (anti-Goodhart). 5) Produces a baseline-vs-migrated checkout pair usable as the R0 repo-delta arm.

title: R0 falsification as a codeprobe experiment (AOA-migrated config arm)
description: Run R0 as a codeprobe EXPERIMENT. repo-delta = a config arm running the agent on the AOA-migrated repo vs baseline repo on the SAME mined tasks. harness-delta = swap agent/harness config, fixed repo. AOA adds the trace-level process deltas codeprobe's outcome-only comparison can't see, plus R0' hardening (K>=3 determinism — expensive given agent non-determinism; convention-invariance; eligibility; power). codeprobe 'experiment aggregate' bias-detection guards the comparison. Smoke on 1 task, scale to >=5 repos, run 'aoa falsify' -> proceed|pivot|inconclusive.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A documented runbook + script stands up a codeprobe experiment with >=2 config arms: baseline repo vs AOA-migrated repo (repo-delta) and an agent/harness-swap arm (harness-delta) on the SAME mined tasks; AOA post-processes both -> metric records -> 'aoa falsify' -> falsification.json. 2) SMOKE: full pipeline runs end-to-end on 1 repo/1 task and emits falsification.json with a verdict field. 3) SCALE: runs on >=5 calibrated repos; R0' hardening exercised on real data (K>=3 determinism, convention-invariance, eligibility, power); non-deterministic agent runs expected to often yield 'inconclusive' (documented, not a bug). 4) codeprobe 'experiment aggregate' bias warnings surfaced alongside the AOA verdict. 5) The verdict (proceed|pivot|inconclusive) is the documented Wave-1 gate.
notes:
PLAN (aoa-dhk: R0 falsification as a codeprobe experiment): All 3 substrate pieces exist — aoa eval run (2lw, per-task records), aoa eval r0b (2ce, run-level leakage), aoa falsify + aoa-falsify crate (R0/R0prime gate consuming a hand-authored FalsifyInput). The GAP is the glue: nothing builds a FalsifyInput (paired-repo evidence) from codeprobe experiment arms, nothing surfaces codeprobe bias_warnings, no runbook/script.
GOAL: make R0 falsification runnable as a codeprobe experiment end-to-end: experiment arms (baseline-repo vs AOA-migrated-repo = repo-delta; harness-swap = harness-delta, SAME mined tasks) -> AOA post-process -> aoa falsify -> falsification.json w/ verdict; codeprobe experiment-aggregate bias warnings surfaced alongside. Smoke proves plumbing on fixtures; live >=5-repo scale = child bead.
APPROACH: (1) NEW commands/falsify_build.rs + EvalCommand::Experiment: inputs = codeprobe experiment dir + operator manifest (repos -> {repo_arm label, harness_arm label, seeds[], calibrated}) + tasks dir; reuse r0b DualScoring (held-out artifact leg) + discover_tasks + aoa_bench::load_task (provenance); join arms by task_id (identical-pair = present+dual in BOTH arms), K RepoRuns over seeds, eligibility{confidence(graph/operator), native_span(provenance), calibrated(manifest)} -> emit FalsifyInput JSON. (2) Promote DualScoring from r0b.rs into shared commands/codeprobe.rs (2nd genuine use), r0b delegates. (3) aoa falsify gains --bias-warnings <aggregate.json>: read codeprobe bias_warnings[], attach to falsification.json as bias_warnings + echo human, ALONGSIDE (never mutating) the AOA verdict (AC4). (4) POWER fix: aoa_falsify::falsify currently hard-errors TooFewRepos (<5); reclassify "too few eligible repos" as a POWER precondition -> verdict=inconclusive+note (matches the crate R0prime doc), so a 1-repo/1-task SMOKE honestly emits falsification.json w/ a verdict field; keep malformed evidence (k_runs<3, repo w/ <k_runs runs) as hard errors.
(5) docs/r0_runbook.md (documented Wave-1 gate, AC5) + scripts/r0_experiment.sh chaining codeprobe experiment run -> aoa eval run per arm -> aoa eval experiment -> aoa falsify --bias-warnings.
FILES: NEW commands/falsify_build.rs; codeprobe.rs (+shared DualScoring); r0b.rs (delegate); falsify.rs (--bias-warnings); eval.rs+mod.rs (dispatch); cli.rs (Experiment args + FalsifyArgs.bias_warnings); aoa-falsify/src/report.rs(+verdict/error) power reclassify; NEW fixtures (experiment dir 3 arms dual scoring.json + manifest + aggregate.json w/ bias_warnings + tasks); tests/cli.rs; NEW scripts/r0_experiment.sh + docs/r0_runbook.md. ~11 files but one cohesive capstone.
TESTS: builder unit (join, identical-pair gate, eligibility map, missing-seed/non-dual fail-loud); SMOKE integ AC2 (fixture exp -> experiment -> falsify -> falsification.json w/ verdict=inconclusive 1-repo); bias surfacing AC4; falsify power-precondition unit (<5 eligible -> inconclusive not Err; malformed still Err).
RISKS: edit_locality/mutation_depth have no per-task source w/o accepted-solution metrics -> derive from metrics layer when graph/repo present else documented honest default (convention floor/ceiling may go vacuous, like 2lw F_edit); changing falsify TooFewRepos->inconclusive touches tested aoa-falsify (update its tests, keep malformed Err); calibrated/confidence partly operator-declared via manifest (document provenance, no fabrication).
OUT OF SCOPE -> child beads: AC3 live >=5-repo scale on real data (non-deterministic live-agent ops, NOT code); auto-calibration/auto-confidence w/o manifest; any change to codeprobe itself.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial — folded in; all findings verified against cited file:line):
C1 (CRITICAL) DO NOT reclassify falsify TooFewRepos->inconclusive. report.rs:46 (<5 repos) is an INPUT-layer structural guard, deliberately distinct (error.rs:5-13) from the verdict-layer eligibility abstention that ALREADY maps <5 ELIGIBLE repos to inconclusive (verdict.rs:32-34,70-80). Softening it makes a 1-repo smoke output byte-identical to a real 5-repo abstention = verdict laundering. FIX: leave aoa-falsify crate UNTOUCHED; the `aoa falsify` COMMAND (falsify.rs) catches Err(TooFewRepos) and emits a DISTINGUISHABLE falsification.json wrapper {verdict:inconclusive, precondition_unmet:"too_few_repos", repos_submitted:n, min_required:5} + non-zero exit (mirror r0b Verdict::Refused shape). Verdict field exists (AC2) but is honest about why. REJECT replicating 1 repo into 5 synthetic (manufactures cross-repo majority from N=1 = validation_theater).
C2 (CRITICAL) Eligibility.calibrated has ZERO producers in crates/ (grep: only test fixtures). Defaulting it true = flips the gate's strongest trust gate (eligibility.rs:11-15) into a rubber stamp. FIX: manifest MUST declare calibrated per repo explicitly; absence => FAIL LOUD or drop-to-ineligible (calibrated:false). NEVER default true. Same rule for confidence: derive from graph IndexQuality when --repo/--scip-index present, else ineligible/explicit-operator-assertion; never default toward High.
H1 (HIGH) re-walk raw scoring.json is CORRECT (eval_run TaskRecord is single-arm by construction: visible_unobserved, counted_as_success=held_out, eval_run.rs:99-102 — cannot carry two arms). ARM-MAPPING TRAP: repo-arm held-out = ARTIFACT leg of MIGRATED config; harness-arm held-out = ARTIFACT leg of HARNESS-SWAP config. BOTH are artifact/held-out legs of DIFFERENT configs — NOT r0b's artifact-vs-direct single-run mapping. Wire + document explicitly. Amend AC1 "metric records" = the FalsifyInput records (PairTask/RepoResult) AOA builds; runbook still runs `aoa eval run` per arm for the process-metric records AC1 nods to.
H2 (HIGH) edit_locality/mutation_depth: 0.5/depth-1 (test fixture vals) are admitted by EVERY default convention -> degrading to them SILENTLY PASSES convention-invariance (opposite of honest). Also eval_run EditLocality is {floor_inflation,ceiling_inflation} struct, no 1:1 scalar map (coercion = type_coercion_hiding_mismatches). FIX: derive real per-task values only when a real source exists (repo checkout + >=2 accepted solutions); else mark convention_inputs_degraded and route to a precondition_unmet wrapper (verdict inconclusive), NEVER substitute midpoints.
H3 (HIGH) bias reader: path is <exp_dir>/reports/aggregate.json (NOT root); record = {kind, severity, message, detail} (severity = "warning"|"informational", load-bearing, bias_detection.py:83-91). Honor severity; treat no_independent_baseline as GATE-BLOCKING (it invalidates an R0 majority); informational = advisory. Build the fixture from a REAL `experiment aggregate` run so the reader cannot drift.
H4 (HIGH) is_identical_pair = present in BOTH arms AND dual_composite+non-errored in BOTH AND SAME held-out provenance in both AND same task definition (ground-truth oracle). Exclude otherwise (delta.rs:25), per-reason notes + unit test each.
M1 builder under `aoa eval experiment` (SRP-correct, consistent w/ eval run+r0b); REJECT `aoa falsify --from-experiment` (two responsibilities). M2 promote DualScoring r0b.rs:52-124 -> shared codeprobe.rs WITH its tests + escape_debug hardening; single-source SCORE_PASS_THRESHOLD (dup in r0b.rs:47 + eval_run.rs:59). M3 keep bool pass/fail thresholding (passed_* then score>=1.0), no soft threshold. M5 runbook MUST document inconclusive(real R0prime abstention) vs precondition_unmet(too_few_repos/degraded) so the Wave-1 gate (AC5) verdict is legible.
SCOPE DECISION: this bead delivers AC1/AC2/AC4/AC5 (builder + bias-surfacing + runbook + script + SMOKE on fixtures). AC3 (live >=5 calibrated repos, real K>=3 determinism, non-deterministic agent ops) -> CHILD BEAD. Bias-surfacing kept in-bead (named AC4, modest) but implemented per H3.

- SECURITY HIGH (falsify.rs render_human): bias_warnings severity/kind/message come VERBATIM from codeprobe's untrusted aggregate.json and were printed unescaped (terminal-injection). Now escape_debug all three + added regression test (falsify_escapes_untrusted_bias_warning_text) asserting raw ESC/BEL never reach stdout. Also escape_join on eligible/excluded repo_ids; escape_debug r.repo_id in builder render (consistency with adjacent task_id hardening).

title: R0b on live data: compose AOA canary over codeprobe's contamination-free oracle
description: Held-out integrity on live data leans on codeprobe's contamination-free mining + consensus/AST oracle + bias detectors (backend_overlap/overshipping/no_independent_baseline). AOA's leakage canary + synthesis-rejection (already in aoa-gap, tested) compose ON TOP as a second independent check. Wire codeprobe oracle/held-out outcomes into aoa_gap::RunResult with correct provenance (External/NativeComposed); a benchmark task lacking an independent held-out leg -> gap:unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) codeprobe oracle/held-out outcomes wired into aoa_gap::RunResult with correct HeldOutProvenance (External/NativeComposed); synthesized-from-visible rejected loud (already in aoa-gap) — tested with a sample. 2) Leakage canary fires on live-shaped data when held-out rises while visible flat and a known held-out item flips (aoa-gap canary composed over codeprobe results) — tested. 3) A task family with no independent held-out leg -> gap:unavailable and refuses a 'good' label — tested. 4) cargo test passes; build green.
notes:
PLAN: Add `aoa eval r0b` — a RUN-LEVEL leakage gate composing aoa_gap::compare over live codeprobe runs. eval_run (aoa-2lw) emits PER-TASK records (single-task RunResults); R0b needs run-level aggregation across many tasks plus an INDEPENDENT visible-vs-held-out signal.
KEY GROUNDING: codeprobe dual-verifier scoring.json (scorer_family=dual_composite) carries details.{passed_direct,passed_artifact,score_direct,score_artifact} + sub_scores. Mapping (per codeprobe prd_dual_verifier_mining.md): visible=DIRECT leg (test.sh, the leg the agent optimizes), held_out=ARTIFACT leg (answer.json vs mined ground_truth = contamination-free oracle). The composite score eval_run reads BLENDS both and cannot drive leakage detection — R0b must read the two legs separately.
APPROACH: (1) per run dir, aggregate -> aoa_gap::RunResult{tasks,held_out_provenance,canaries}: discover task dirs, read both dual legs per task, load oracle (bench) for per-task provenance. (2) run provenance precedence: None if ANY task None (->Unavailable, AC3); else External if all External; else NativeComposed (documented deterministic rule). (3) canaries from --canary manifest [{id,expected_held_out}], observed held_out from the run. (4) aoa_gap::compare(baseline,migrated) -> map GapError loud: LeakageDetected/GapUnavailable/SynthesizedHeldOut => non-zero exit; CompareOutcome(Good/NotGood)=>exit0; JSON+human. (5) a run task lacking BOTH dual legs fails loud (R0b needs an independent visible leg; never fabricated).
FILES: cli.rs (+R0b(R0bArgs){baseline,migrated,tasks,canary?,json}); commands/codeprobe.rs NEW (extract shared discover_tasks + Scoring w/ dual-leg accessor); commands/eval_run.rs (use shared); commands/r0b.rs NEW (aggregate+compare+output); commands/eval.rs (dispatch); commands/mod.rs; fixtures codeprobe_run_baseline/ + _migrated/ (dual scoring.json) + r0b_canary.json + a None-provenance task; tests/cli.rs (AC1/2/3).
TESTS: AC1 r0b over a sample run -> RunResult provenance External/NativeComposed (gap available). AC2 baseline-vs-migrated: artifact(held_out) rises, direct(visible) flat, canary flips -> non-zero + "leakage". AC3 tasks set w/ a None task -> gap unavailable -> non-zero, refuses good. AC1 synthesized-rejected clause already covered by aoa-gap unit tests; our path routes through compute_gap, cannot bypass.
RISKS: direct/artifact->visible/held-out mapping is a semantic choice (grounded, flag for review); float-exact visible_rate equality for visible_flat (fixtures use identical direct legs); run-level provenance precedence is new policy (documented).
OUT OF SCOPE: changing eval_run composite held_out source; auto-discovering canaries; non-dual single-leg runs (fail loud); multi-config layouts.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial — folded in):
Q1 (CRITICAL, visible=DIRECT category error): KEEP visible=DIRECT(test.sh) / held_out=ARTIFACT(answer.json vs mined ground_truth), but REFRAME + CONSTRAIN. Rationale: aoa_gap::compare REQUIRES a visible signal for BOTH leakage_detected.visible_flat AND gap-delta labeling; leaving visible unobserved makes AC2 untestable and labeling degenerate, so a visible leg is NECESSARY for the ACs. Resolution: (a) DIRECT is the gameable PROXY verifier (runnable test on the agent diff), ARTIFACT is closer to the true objective (full mined change) — gap=proxy_pass-true_pass is a legit reward-hacking signature; DOC that "visible" here = the runnable-test proxy leg, NOT "spec text the agent literally sees" (avoid the validation-theater claim aoa-2lw rejected). (b) Only aggregate genuinely-dual tasks (scorer_family==dual_composite, both legs ran w/o error_direct/error_artifact) — avoids the absent-leg fail-open (comprehension tasks w/o test.sh pinning DIRECT=0.0).
Q2 (HIGH, canary cannot fire on live path): CONFIRMED — to_run_result/eval_run hardcode canaries:[]; r0b MUST build the run-level RunResult DIRECTLY (not via to_run_result) and inject CanaryItem{id,held_out_success:observed,expected_held_out:from-manifest}. expected_held_out is OPERATOR-declared in --canary manifest (a known probe w/ known clean expectation) — honest, not tool-fabricated. The visible_flat EXACT-f64 fail-open (a leak that nudges DIRECT by one task suppresses detection) is a defect of the SHIPPED aoa_gap::leakage_detected algorithm, NOT this beads scope (AC2 is scoped to that exact condition); fixtures construct the signature precisely (DIRECT identical across baseline/migrated). FILE FOLLOW-UP: tolerance/canary-driven leakage detection.
Q3 (HIGH, aggregation must be exhaustive): run-provenance reduce is 4-way over HeldOutProvenance: Synthesized=>Err(SynthesizedHeldOut); else any None=>None(->Unavailable,AC3); else all External=>External; else NativeComposed. No "otherwise" bucket that could launder Synthesized.
Q4 (MED, AC1 synthesized test): add (1) unit test classify_provenance never emits Synthesized for codeprobe inputs; (2) unit test the new run-level aggregation REJECTS a Synthesized member (Err) — tests AC1 rejection at the live-wiring layer, not just compute_gap.
Q5 (MED): new subcommand confirmed; r0b is a THIN composition over aoa_gap::compare (no reimpl of leakage math). Keep name r0b (project domain vocabulary: bead title is "R0b on live data"; R-codes are the projects terms).
Q6 (MED, consume-output vs re-walk): RE-WALK raw run dirs in r0b (NOT consume eval run JSON) — eval_run deliberately does NOT surface the dual legs (visible_unobserved); r0b needs scoring.json directly for passed_direct/passed_artifact. This justifies extracting discover_tasks into commands/codeprobe.rs (genuine 2nd use); eval_run delegates (small safe change).
Q7 (MED, fail-loud + Scoring fields): r0b FAILS LOUD on a non-dual run (scorer_family != dual_composite) naming the cause; eval_run KEEPS degrading to gap:unavailable (unchanged). r0b Scoring extends to deserialize scorer_family + details.{score_direct,score_artifact,passed_direct,passed_artifact} (eval_run.rs:61-67 reads only score/passed — insufficient for the two legs).
NEW FOLLOW-UP BEADS to file: (1) leakage_detected visible_flat tolerance/canary-driven hardening; (2) revisit visible-leg semantics doc across eval_run+r0b for consistency.
PROGRESS: implemented `aoa eval r0b` run-level leakage gate. New: commands/r0b.rs (DualScoring reads scorer_family+direct/artifact legs from codeprobe dual_composite scoring.json; aggregate_provenance exhaustive 4-way reduce; aggregate_run -> run-level RunResult w/ canaries injected directly; compare -> Labeled/Refused, non-zero on leakage/unavailable/synthesized), commands/codeprobe.rs (shared discover_tasks extracted from eval_run, eval_run now delegates), cli.rs R0bArgs+R0b variant, eval.rs dispatch, mod.rs. Fixtures: r0b_run_baseline/_migrated (dual, leakage signature), r0b_canary.json, r0b_tasks_unavailable+r0b_run_unavailable (None provenance), r0b_run_singleleg (non-dual). Tests: 6 unit (provenance reduce incl synthesized-reject, non-dual/errored-leg loud) + 5 integration (AC1 provenance+available, AC2 leakage fires, AC3 unavailable refuses label, non-dual fail-loud, human render). Full workspace green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
REVIEW: unified Phase-4 (code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer) on the r0b diff. 0 CRITICAL. Fixed in-bead: (sec) untrusted task_id + scoring.json error strings (error_direct/error_artifact) now escape_debug in all bail! messages, matching eval_run terminal-injection hardening; (code/rust) aggregate_provenance now bails loud on an empty provenance slice (was silently returning External); (rust) load_canary_manifest drops needless spec.id clone via contains_key-then-insert; (rust) discover_tasks pub -> pub(crate); (rust/code) canary-membership validation hoisted before per-task I/O so a bad canary id fails fast with no wasted reads. PUSHED BACK (kept as-is w/ clarifying comment): synthesized-from-visible stays a HARD anyhow error (not routed through Verdict::Refused JSON) — it cannot arise from codeprobe data (classify_provenance never emits it), so reaching it = upstream corruption / invariant violation, which must NOT masquerade as an expected gate refusal (leakage/unavailable) a CI tool treats as routine data. DEFERRED (pre-existing, tracked): zero-SHA->External loader gap (aoa-d6t.2 NOTES); unbounded scoring.json read + aoa-bench backend_variant_files symlink-follow (folded into aoa-d6t.1 scope); leakage_detected exact-f64 visible_flat fail-open (new follow-up aoa filed). 8 unit + 26 integration green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
close_reason: DONE: implemented `aoa eval r0b`, a run-level R0b held-out-integrity gate composing aoa_gap::compare over two live codeprobe runs. held_out=ARTIFACT(mined ground_truth) leg, visible=DIRECT(test.sh) gameable-proxy leg from dual_composite scoring.json; exhaustive 4-way provenance reduce (synthesized=>hard error, any None=>gap:unavailable refuses label, all-External=>External, else NativeComposed); canaries injected from operator --canary manifest; refusal=>non-zero exit. Extracted shared discover_tasks (commands/codeprobe.rs). All 5 ACs met (AC1 provenance+available, AC2 leakage fires, AC3 unavailable refuses label, AC4 green). 6 unit + 26 integration tests; clippy -D warnings + fmt clean. Phase-4 fixes: escape untrusted task_id/error strings, fail-loud empty-provenance guard, drop clone, pub(crate), hoist canary validation; kept synthesized as hard-error (invariant violation, not routine refusal). Key decision: DIRECT/ARTIFACT->visible/held-out mapping grounded in codeprobe dual-verifier PRD. Follow-ups filed: aoa-d6t.1 (unbounded read + loader symlink), aoa-d6t.6 (leakage_detected visible_flat fail-open). Merged on main at cb48fd4635d1e589fd8ff542783841553b92a277.

title: aoa eval run: post-process a codeprobe run -> AOA metric records
description: aoa eval run POST-PROCESSES a codeprobe run (it does NOT orchestrate an agent — codeprobe does). After 'codeprobe run', for each CompletedTask: feed agent_stdout through the trace-shim (aoa-b4w) -> 8-span trace; build the SCIP graph (aoa-671) over the task repo; join to populate symbol.lookup + G_t + I_t; compute the 4 process metrics + the reward-hacking gap (held-out from codeprobe's oracle). Emit per-task AOA metric records ALONGSIDE codeprobe's outcome scores. Depends on b4w + 671 + cpz.
acceptance_criteria: 1) 'aoa eval run --codeprobe-run <runs/.../dir>' reads each trial's agent_output.txt, runs the shim (aoa-b4w)->trace, builds the graph (aoa-671) over the task repo, loads task/oracle (aoa-cpz), emits a per-task AOA metric record (4 metrics + reward-hacking gap); test against a committed sample codeprobe run dir. 2) Dual-register output: human + --json (R17), consistent with existing aoa CLI. 3) Records carry conditioned_on=held_out and the gap from codeprobe's oracle; visible-pass-but-held-out-fail NOT counted as success. 4) A missing/invalid transcript for a trial fails loud for that trial (reported), never silently skipped. 5) cargo test -p aoa passes; build green.
notes:
PLAN: New 'aoa eval run --codeprobe-run <run_dir> [--tasks <tasks_dir>] [--json]' subcommand post-processing a codeprobe run.
CONTRACT (verified in codeprobe core/executor.py:_save_task_artifacts): run_dir/<task_id>/{agent_output.txt (secret-sanitized stdout), scoring.json (score==reward in [0,1], status, sub_scores)}. Task defs/oracle live in the codeprobe TASK SOURCE dir (metadata.json|task.toml + tests/ground_truth.json) read via aoa_bench::load_task -> needs a --tasks companion arg; codeprobe score = held-out oracle (mined ground truth, held-out by construction).
APPROACH: (1) CLI: additive — EvalArgs gains optional #[command(subcommand)] Option<EvalCommand>{Run(EvalRunArgs)}; legacy --validate-trace/--compare flags unchanged so the 4 existing cli tests don't break. (2) aoa/Cargo.toml += path deps aoa-codeprobe-shim, aoa-scip-graph, aoa-bench, aoa-metrics. (3) commands/eval_run.rs: per <task_id> subdir -> parse_transcript_file (FAIL LOUD per-task on missing/invalid, never skip) -> Trace; read scoring.json -> held_out_success; load_task(<tasks_dir>/<id>) -> gold_set/accepted_solutions/provenance + repo; build_symbol_graph(BestEffort{repo_dir}) (Degraded if repo absent -> zero weight, already enforced); assemble MetricInput{trace,gold_set,invariant_set,transform:default,edited_files,accepted_solutions,graph,k,held_out_success}; compute_metrics -> MetricRecord; compute_gap(task.to_run_result(visible,held_out)) -> GapOutcome (Unavailable when provenance None). (4) Emit per-task record view (4 metrics + gap + conditioned_on=HeldOut + counted_as_success) dual-register via output::{print_human,print_json}.
SCOPE NOTE: ~6 files but cohesive -> single bead MVP. symbol.lookup join DEFERRED (needs SCIP graph wiring, documented, aoa-671). edited_files derived from write.attempt span targets in the trace.
RISKS: visible-success source in scoring.json is ambiguous -> MVP uses held_out from oracle score, visible from a scoring sub_score if present else trace-has-passing-test.run; task repo checkout may be absent in run dir -> graph degrades (supported). 
OUT OF SCOPE: symbol.lookup population (aoa-671 follow-up), R0b canary (aoa-2ce), R0 experiment (aoa-dhk).
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial): revised plan, folding in findings.
- C1 (CRITICAL): MetricInput.edited_files (F_edit) has NO source in a codeprobe run dir (no patch/diff retained). MVP: derive F_edit = path attrs on write.attempt/write.blocked spans. Consequence: trivial probe transcripts (prose answers, zero writes) -> empty F_edit -> degenerate edit-locality/mutation-surface. File follow-up: git-diff-based F_edit.
- C2 (CRITICAL): graph needs a repo checkout the run dir does NOT retain; index_best_effort is Python-ONLY and returns empty G_t/I_t. MVP: graph source via explicit optional --scip-index <path> OR --repo <checkout>; absent -> Degraded (zero weight, R0-ineligible) but LOGGED, never silent. gold_set from aoa_bench oracle; invariant_set has no bench source -> empty (documented vacuous). File follow-ups: non-Python best-effort; invariant_set source.
- H1 (HIGH): visible_success has no real source (scoring.json has no sub_scores; shim test.run carries no exit status). Do NOT fabricate from oracle score (validation_theater). MVP: held_out_success from scoring.passed/score; visible_success set = held_out with visible_unobserved=true flag; gap only claimed when a genuine separate visible suite exists.
- H2 (HIGH): real layout is run_dir/<config_label>/<task_id>/... ; --codeprobe-run points at the config-label dir; handle/scope the config-label layer explicitly.
- H3 (HIGH): real task dirs (task.toml-only) DON'T satisfy load_task (needs instruction.md+ground_truth.json) -> AC#1 fixture is HAND-ASSEMBLED; join run-trial->task-dir by task_id name; verify join key (hash-keyed runs need a map -> follow-up).
- M1: CLI -> nest enum EvalCommand{ValidateTrace,Compare,Run(EvalRunArgs)} mirroring PolicyCommand (small refactor of existing eval, in-scope), NOT a 3rd optional flag. Update the 4 existing cli tests to the nested surface.
- M2: add path deps aoa-bench, aoa-codeprobe-shim, aoa-scip-graph, aoa-metrics to crates/aoa/Cargo.toml.
- M3: fail-loud = per-trial ERROR RECORD + continue; command exits non-zero if any trial errored; JSON carries records[] + errors[] with root cause.
- M4: keep ONE bead (pure composition); hard boundary — do NOT add patch-extraction/multi-lang indexing here; file as follow-ups.
PROGRESS: implemented commands/eval_run.rs (orchestrator) + nested EvalCommand{ValidateTrace,Compare,Run} CLI (mirrors PolicyCommand) + 4 path deps. Honest degradation per architect review: F_edit from write-spans, graph degrades+logs without --scip-index/--repo, visible_unobserved flag, edit-locality null on <2 solutions, per-trial fail-loud error records + nonzero exit. Tests: 5 new eval-run cli tests + updated 4 existing eval tests to nested surface = 21 cli tests pass; full workspace green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
REVIEW: unified Phase-4 (code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer) on the eval-run diff. 0 CRITICAL. 3 HIGH all FIXED in-wave: (1) symlink-follow in discover_tasks -> DirEntry::file_type (no symlink traversal); (2) MetricError match forward-compat -> documented intentional exhaustiveness (new variants become compile errors, never silent null); (3) AC4 missing-TRANSCRIPT path untested+unhandled -> discovery now keys on scoring.json OR agent_output.txt so a transcript-less trial is discovered and fails loud (new broken-no-transcript fixture + test). MEDIUM fixed: shim.warnings surfaced as transcript_warnings (was dropped); reuse bench to_run_result for provenance stamping (was duplicated); task_count->record_count (naming); graph_quality typed IndexQuality not &str. LOW fixed: task_id escape_debug in human output (terminal-injection); early stderr warning when no graph source; removed dead (None,None) edit arm. Deferred: unbounded-alloc (aoa-d6t.1, eval_run noted as entry point); zero-SHA->External loader gap (aoa-d6t.2); MetricInput per-task clones (new P3 bead). 21 cli tests + full workspace green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean.
close_reason: DONE: 'aoa eval run' post-processes a codeprobe run into per-task AOA metric records (4 process metrics + reward-hacking gap), composing the shim/scip-graph/bench/metrics/gap crates behind a nested EvalCommand. Honest-degradation MVP per architect review (F_edit from write-spans, degrade-on-no-graph-source, visible_unobserved, edit-locality null on <2 solutions, per-trial fail-loud + nonzero exit). All 5 ACs met; 21 cli tests; clippy/fmt clean. Follow-ups: aoa-d6t.1/.2/.5. Merged on main at 5833710eb7f2eb18460a2db20be88fae0476aa64.

title: scip-graph: real SCIP/tree-sitter index -> aoa_metrics::SymbolGraph + R15 tiering
description: Replace the in-crate fixture SymbolGraph with a real index of the target repo. SCIP-grade -> high-confidence; tree-sitter/stack-graphs best-effort -> low-confidence (R15). Populates the graph, gold set G_t, and invariant set I_t. A degraded/empty index lowers weight and disqualifies the repo from R0 voting (already enforced in aoa-metrics).

title: trace-shim: codeprobe agent_stdout (stream-json) -> 8-span AOA trace
description:
Parse a codeprobe per-trial transcript into a native 8-span AOA trace.

INPUT: codeprobe writes the full agent transcript to <runs_dir>/<task_id>/agent_output.txt per trial (codeprobe executor.py:953, secret-sanitized). It is newline-delimited JSON because codeprobe runs `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose` (codeprobe adapters/claude.py:393). Each line is a JSON object; assistant messages hold content blocks; a tool call is a block {"type":"tool_use","name":<Tool>,"input":{...}}; the stream ends with a {"type":"result"} event. codeprobe src/codeprobe/adapters/telemetry.py::_parse_stream_json already parses these blocks but only COUNTS them by name (tool_use_by_name) — AOA must instead keep ORDER and TARGETS.

OUTPUT: an aoa_trace::Trace (source=native) passing aoa_trace::validate_trace, with monotonic seq.

MAPPING: Grep/Glob/search-type tools -> retrieval.search (target=query); Read -> file.read (target=path); Edit/Write/MultiEdit -> write.attempt (target=path); Bash running tests (pytest/test.sh) -> test.run; MCP tools (name starts mcp__) -> gateway.invoke; a tool_use followed by a permission-denied/blocked result -> write.blocked; a trial that ends with no Edit/Write -> abstain. symbol.lookup is populated by joining tool results to the SCIP graph (aoa-671) and MAY be absent until that lands (document it).
acceptance_criteria: 1) crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim parses a committed real agent_output.txt fixture and emits an aoa_trace::Trace passing aoa_trace::validate_trace (test). 2) Spans preserve transcript ORDER (monotonic seq) and TARGETS (path/query/symbol in attributes) — asserted by tests. 3) Tool->span mapping covers the cases in the description; symbol.lookup may be absent pre-graph (documented); write.blocked emitted on a denied tool result. 4) All spans source=native. 5) Unknown/unmapped tool names handled explicitly (logged/mapped to a documented default), NEVER silently swallowed. 6) A sanitized sample agent_output.txt is committed as a fixture so the test runs without codeprobe. 7) cargo test -p aoa-codeprobe-shim passes; cargo build --workspace green.
notes:
LOCATIONS (cross-repo — a fresh session needs these):
- AOA repo (work lands here): /home/ds/projects/aoa — Rust workspace, members = crates/*. Add a NEW crate crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim with a path dep on aoa-trace. Read crates/aoa-trace/src for the API: SpanType (8 discriminants: retrieval.search/file.read/symbol.lookup/write.attempt/write.blocked/test.run/gateway.invoke/abstain), SpanSource (Native|Reconstructed), Span (has seq + attributes), Trace, validate_trace.
- codeprobe repo (the SUBSTRATE, SEPARATE project): /home/ds/projects/codeprobe (Python). It orchestrates the agent and writes per-trial artifacts under runs/<run-id>/<task_id>/ (agent_output.txt = the transcript; scoring.json; etc.).
- Produce a sample: in ~/projects/codeprobe run a task (see its README quick start: `codeprobe run <dir> --agent claude`), then read runs/.../<task_id>/agent_output.txt. Or reuse an existing transcript already under ~/projects/codeprobe/runs/. Commit a sanitized sample as the test fixture so the shim test runs WITHOUT codeprobe present.

VERIFIED 2026-06-19: codeprobe runs Claude stream-json --verbose (adapters/claude.py:393); transcript retained AgentOutput.stdout -> CompletedTask.agent_stdout -> written to disk as agent_output.txt (executor.py:952-955). No change to codeprobe's run path needed — AOA consumes what it already persists.
PLAN: New crate crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim, path dep on aoa-trace. Parse newline-delimited stream-json (claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose). Walk events in order: type=assistant -> message.content blocks; tool_use block {name,input,id} -> map to SpanType keeping ORDER (monotonic seq) + TARGET in attributes. type=user -> message.content tool_result blocks {tool_use_id,is_error,content}; is_error+permission/denied text on a write tool -> reclassify that tool_use to write.blocked. Mapping: Grep/Glob/search* -> retrieval.search (attr query); Read -> file.read (attr path); Edit/Write/MultiEdit -> write.attempt (attr path); Bash w/ test cmd (pytest/test.sh) -> test.run; mcp__* -> gateway.invoke; trial ending with no Edit/Write -> append abstain span. Unknown tools -> documented default (gateway.invoke if mcp-like else logged+skipped? -> map to a default + log, NEVER swallow). symbol.lookup absent pre-graph (documented). All source=native, seq increments per emitted span. Files: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, src/parse.rs, src/mapping.rs, tests/fixture.rs, tests/fixtures/agent_output.txt. Tests: order+targets, each mapping case, write.blocked, abstain, unknown-tool handling, validate_trace passes. Risks: exact tool_result blocked shape; Bash test detection heuristic (ZFC: keep mechanical+documented).
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Unknown tools: do NOT map to a default span type (pollutes trace) — instead skip emitting a span but RECORD the name in a returned warnings Vec so it is logged, never silently swallowed (AC5). Return (Trace, Vec<warning>) from parse. (2) write.blocked needs lookahead: tool_use emits write.attempt span; matching tool_result with is_error reclassifies that span in place via tool_use_id->index map. Keeps ORDER. (3) Bash test.run detection is a documented mechanical heuristic (command contains pytest/test.sh/cargo test/go test/npm test) — ZFC-acceptable since spec explicitly prescribes it; document it. (4) abstain emitted once at end iff no write.attempt span exists. (5) seq monotonic non-decreasing — use strictly increasing counter. (6) Empty/blank lines and non-JSON lines skipped (matches codeprobe parser). Decision: keep parse.rs (event walk) + mapping.rs (tool->SpanType pure fn) separate for SRP.

title: R0 falsification SCALE: live run on >=5 calibrated repos (AC3 of aoa-dhk)
description: Execute the R0 falsification experiment (built in aoa-dhk) on >=5 calibrated repos with real, non-deterministic agent runs (K>=3 determinism, convention-invariance, eligibility, power exercised on REAL data). Ops/data-collection campaign, not code: run codeprobe experiment arms live (claude -p), AOA post-process via scripts/r0_experiment.sh, capture falsification.json verdicts. Non-deterministic runs expected to often yield 'inconclusive' (documented, not a bug). The runbook (docs/r0_runbook.md) from aoa-dhk documents the procedure.
notes:
PLAN (prep-only, no-API per user): SCOPE = all R0 live-campaign setup that costs ZERO API; STOP before codeprobe run/calibrate/mine-with-LLM. MIGRATION DEFN (recommended): repo-delta treatment = AOA-clean agent-context closure (aoa lint-context passes from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md + aoa audit no Tier-1 gaps); baseline arm = same repo+harness WITHOUT the AOA-clean context (code identical); harness-swap arm = baseline repo + varied harness (model/tool/sysprompt). Validity guardrails: migration must be EXACTLY the repo-resident context surface aoa touches and nothing the harness arm also varies (else repo-delta/harness-delta confound); SCIP index = manifest 'confidence' eligibility input NOT the treatment; baseline differs from migrated ONLY in context closure. DELIVERABLES (all zero-API): (1) fix runbook drift 'codeprobe experiment run'->real 'codeprobe run' per-config procedure; (2) experiment scaffold via experiment init+add-config (3 arms, filesystem only); (3) manifest template w/ confidence+calibrated placeholders + arm paths; (4) mine --no-llm template tasks for >=1 repo to give real task dirs w/o API; (5) re-validate AOA post-process chain (scripts/r0_experiment.sh) green on fixtures; (6) document the deferred live-run command sequence + cost estimate. DEFER to a live session: codeprobe run (agent), codeprobe calibrate (curator model), mine w/ LLM enrichment, the >=5-repo K>=3 campaign.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect, adversarial — REJECTED the context-file migration defn): CRITICAL-1: CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md are prompt/harness material, not the code layer — putting them in repo-delta arm CONFOUNDS with harness-delta arm (both vary 'effective agent context'), which is exactly the separation R0 exists to make. A 'proceed' would license only 'good agent instructions help' (a harness finding), not 'the repo is the right layer'. CRITICAL-2: Goodhart loop — AOA defines 'migrated' (lint-context pass + audit clean) AND AOA's gate scores whether 'migrated' wins; treatment+criterion share an author -> not falsifiable. HIGH-1: prose context files can encode held-out oracle answers (asymmetric contamination inflating repo-delta only). HIGH-2: the two deltas aren't independent (shared mediator = agent context quality). Architect resolutions: (1) re-label AOA's intervention as context-LAYER, equalize context channel across arms so only WHERE context lives differs (durable repo files vs ephemeral harness); or (2) code-LAYER structural treatment (changes compiler/tests/graph see). NEW EVIDENCE against resolution-2: 'aoa audit --json' surfaces only ENFORCEMENT-PLANE gaps (CI/pre-commit/writable-mutation), NOT code-structure — AOA has no tool producing a code-semantics treatment. CONCLUSION: the migration definition is unresolved; prep built on the context-file defn would rest on a construct-invalid foundation. Decision escalated to user.
RESOLUTION (user, design): R0's claimed layer = code-infrastructure best-practices (structure/naming/organization/modularity + navigable in-repo docs the agent builds on), NOT the harness/context layer. AOA = observability guardrail that audits adherence + executes migrations to restore best-practices. This resolves the architect's construct-validity rejection: the repo-delta treatment is now CODE-LAYER (compiler/tests/graph/navigation see it), distinct from harness, oracle-blind, spec-defined (anti-Goodhart). PREP DONE this session (zero-API): (1) docs/r0_runbook.md — added 'What counts as the AOA migration (repo-delta treatment)' section w/ the 3 guardrails + capability note; fixed the drift bug (no 'codeprobe experiment run'; real flow is experiment init/add-config + top-level 'codeprobe run --config <dir>' w/ --dry-run/--max-cost-usd); documented that add-config sets HARNESS knobs only so repo dimension = repo STATE tasks run against. (2) Filed aoa-mnz (P1) = the audit+migrate capability that is the precondition for a construct-valid repo-delta arm; aoa-dhk.1 now BLOCKED on it. BLOCKED until aoa-mnz delivers a tool-driven, reproducible, code-layer treatment. Live ops campaign (codeprobe run, calibrate, mine-w/-LLM, >=5 repos K>=3) deferred per user (prep-only, no-API).

title: Harden aoa_gap leakage_detected: visible_flat exact-f64 equality fails open
description: aoa-gap/src/compare.rs:51 leakage_detected uses migrated.visible_rate()==baseline.visible_rate() (exact f64). A real leak that ALSO nudges the visible (DIRECT) leg by one task out of many makes visible_flat=false and SUPPRESSES detection (fail-open). Surfaced composing the canary over live codeprobe data in aoa-2ce (fixtures construct the exact-flat signature, so AC2 passes, but production runs rarely have perfectly-flat visible). Add a tolerance band (e.g. |delta|<=eps) OR drive detection off canary items alone when the visible leg is noisy. Acceptance: a leak with a 1/N visible nudge still trips; tests; build green.
notes:
PLAN: Fix fail-open in aoa-gap leakage_detected (compare.rs:51). Replace exact-f64 'visible_flat = migrated.visible_rate()==baseline.visible_rate()' with a delta comparison. New rule: trip when held_out_delta>0.0 AND held_out_delta>visible_delta AND canary_flipped — i.e. held-out rose and OUTPACED visible, confirmed by a canary flip. No magic eps. Degrades to original when visible flat (visible_delta==0 -> held_out_delta>0); fails CLOSED on a 1/N visible nudge (held-out still strictly outpaces); honest broad gain (visible keeps pace) does NOT trip. Files: src/compare.rs (leakage_detected + doc), tests/criteria.rs (+ regression test: leak with 1/N visible nudge still trips). Existing criterion_3_leakage_canary_fails + criterion_3_no_false_positive stay green. Risk: borderline honest gain where held-out outpaces visible AND a canary legitimately flips would trip — accepted (fail-closed is the bead's intent). Out of scope: other epic children, eval/CLI wiring.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect): original 'held_out_delta>visible_delta' REJECTED — it's the labeling comparison misapplied to integrity logic and relocates the fail-open (large visible gain would mask a flipped canary). Architect's 'canary-alone' ALSO rejected: over-trips on noisy/nondeterministic canary flips; the aggregate guard is a deliberate corroboration check the bead's 'visible leg is noisy' framing depends on. ADOPTED: tolerance band (bead option A). visible_flat := |migrated.visible_rate()-baseline.visible_rate()| <= 1/min(N) (one task's granularity; symmetric). Keep held_out_delta>0.0 && canary_flipped. Reduces to original when N tasks share the same set and visible truly flat; trips on a +-1/N nudge; large visible gain (broad honest improvement, outside band) deliberately NOT flagged. Tests: (1) leak w/ 1/N visible-DOWN nudge trips [regression], (2) 1/N visible-UP nudge trips, (3) broad visible gain + flipped canary NOT flagged [pins the scope decision].
PROGRESS: implemented tolerance-band fix in compare.rs leakage_detected (visible_flat = |delta|<=1/min(N)); added 3 regression tests. aoa-gap suite 10/10 green (2 new nudge tests RED->GREEN; broad-gain + no_false_positive still pass).
REVIEW (3 parallel agents: code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer): all APPROVE, 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH. Recurring MEDIUM = coverage gap on min(N) unequal-task-count + N=1 boundary -> ADDRESSED by adding criterion_3_band_uses_smaller_task_count and criterion_3_single_task_trips_on_canary_flip (now 12 tests). Reviewers hand-verified all new tests' arithmetic and the original-behavior degradation. REJECTED 'distinct canary ids' (LOW): shared id correctly models the SAME canary across baseline/migrated, matching pre-existing criterion_3_leakage_canary_fails. Security MEDIUM 'contamination passes silently when no canary present' is pre-existing by-design (operator-injected canaries) — filed as tracking follow-up. clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; my lines fmt-clean (only pre-existing criterion_6 drift remains -> aoa-mqw).
close_reason: DONE: Replaced exact-f64 visible_flat equality in aoa-gap leakage_detected with a 1/min(N) tolerance band, closing the fail-open where a 1/N visible nudge suppressed leakage detection. Kept held_out_rose && canary_flipped corroboration (rejected canary-alone: over-fires on nondeterministic flips). 12 tests green (5 new), clippy -D warnings clean. Filed aoa-d6t.7 for the pre-existing canary-absence fail-open. Merged on main at f81f713f9140e52e4efcb0a9df31198b8587db7e.
