title: provenance: track backend identity, not file-set cardinality, for NativeComposed
description: Phase-4 code review of aoa-cpz. classify_provenance (aoa-bench/src/provenance.rs:21) dedups accepted_solutions by BTreeSet file-set EQUALITY, not by backend identity. Two backends with identical file-sets collapse to one entry (no NativeComposed); a one-file difference yields NativeComposed. The doc says 'two or more mined backends that INDEPENDENTLY agreed' — independence is about backend provenance, which is discarded at load time (loader read_ground_truth drops the ground_truth_<backend>.json stem). A reviewer can't audit whether NativeComposed reflects two genuine independent mining runs. Either carry backend names alongside each accepted solution, or document the operational definition explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: 1) accepted_solutions carry backend identity (e.g. from ground_truth_<backend>.json stem) OR provenance doc explicitly states the file-set-distinctness operational definition. 2) NativeComposed decision is auditable from the loaded structure. 3) tests updated; build green.
notes:
Related loader gap found in aoa-2lw review: aoa-bench loader accepts an all-zeros ground_truth_commit as a real commit (only blank strings filtered), so a synthetic task classifies as External provenance. Reject/flag null SHAs when wiring backend-identity provenance.
PLAN: Make NativeComposed reflect backend IDENTITY, not file-set cardinality.
ROOT CAUSE: accepted_solutions is Vec<BTreeSet<String>> (file-sets only); classify_provenance (provenance.rs:21) dedups by file-set EQUALITY -> 2 backends with identical files collapse to None (false negative; identical agreement is the STRONGEST independence signal), while a 1-file diff grants NativeComposed. Backend identity (ground_truth_<backend>.json stem) is dropped at load.
APPROACH:
- New AcceptedSolution { backend: String, files: BTreeSet<String> } in aoa-bench (exported); CodeprobeTask.accepted_solutions: Vec<AcceptedSolution>.
- loader.read_ground_truth: canonical oracle set -> backend 'canonical'; each ground_truth_<backend>.json -> filename stem.
- provenance: NativeComposed when DISTINCT backend identities >=2 (BTreeSet<&str>), else None; External path unchanged. Auditable: backend names live on the loaded task.
- bridge.edit_locality_anchors + eval_run MetricInput: map AcceptedSolution -> file-sets; aoa-metrics MetricInput stays Vec<BTreeSet> (untouched boundary).
- Tests: provenance unit tests (2 backends identical files -> NativeComposed [the fix]; 2 diff -> NativeComposed; 1 -> None; external -> External); update loader_test.
FILES (~7): task.rs, lib.rs, loader.rs, provenance.rs, bridge.rs, eval_run.rs, loader_test.rs.
VERIFIED count semantics preserve fixtures: native-consensus-001 (canonical+ast=2 -> NativeComposed), no-heldout-002 (0 -> None), externals via commit.
OUT OF SCOPE (-> follow-up bead): null/all-zeros ground_truth_commit rejection (NOTE/aoa-2lw) — touches 2 separate fixture trees (bench + aoa/cli) + cli.rs + exact-SHA assertions; distinct concern; file as P2 sub-bead.
DISCOVERY (blocks the planned approach): the bead (and the AOA loader) assume per-backend ground truth lives in 'ground_truth_<backend>.json' files. Verified against codeprobe source (/home/ds/projects/codeprobe): that file pattern is FICTIONAL — codeprobe never emits it. Real multi-backend evidence is 'divergence_report.json' (schema consensus.v1, mining/writer.py:1147), carrying backend_results[].{backend,files,available,error}, consensus_files, pair_metrics, and a 'decision'=shipped/quarantined where shipped == >=2 backends agreed (pairwise F1, consensus.py ConsensusDecision). The agreed answer is written as the single ground_truth.json (= the consensus, not one backend). CONSEQUENCE: AOA's backend_variant_files() reads a format codeprobe doesn't produce; the only ground_truth_<backend>.json files in existence are AOA's own invented fixtures. On REAL codeprobe data there are zero variants -> NativeComposed never fires. The classifier's NativeComposed path is fixture-only fiction today. The bead's true intent (criterion 2: 'auditable from >=2 genuine independent mining runs') is EXACTLY what divergence_report.json provides authoritatively. Correct fix = read divergence_report.json (backend_results + shipped), not ground_truth_<backend>.json stems. This redirects the bead; surfaced to user for a direction call. Architect review also flagged (independently valid regardless of source): HIGH-1 identical file-sets must dedup for the edit-locality view (else degenerate floor==ceiling, regression from a loud InsufficientAcceptedSolutions); MEDIUM-3 stale doc comments in task.rs:28/provenance.rs:9/lib.rs:13/loader.rs:160.
RE-PLAN (user approved 're-ground on divergence_report.json'): The re-grounding DISSOLVES architect CRITICAL-1 (no canonical-as-backend) and CRITICAL-2 (no stem parsing — backend names come from backend_results[].backend); codeprobe's decision=='shipped' is the authoritative >=2-agreement signal (ZFC: consume the tool's decision, don't re-derive F1).
SCHEMA (consensus.v1, codeprobe mining/consensus.py _build_divergence_report, stable/additive): { decision: 'shipped'|'quarantined', backend_results: [{backend, available, n_files, files:[...], error}], consensus_files:[...], pair_metrics, threshold, mode }. Written at task_dir/divergence_report.json (writer.py:1147) for BOTH shipped and quarantined tasks.
DESIGN:
- AcceptedSolution { backend: String, files: BTreeSet<String> } in aoa-bench (exported); CodeprobeTask.accepted_solutions: Vec<AcceptedSolution>.
- loader: NEW read_accepted_backends(dir) parses divergence_report.json; only when decision=='shipped', collect AcceptedSolution for each backend_result with available && error==null && !files.empty() && !backend.empty(). REMOVE backend_variant_files + the fictional ground_truth_<backend>.json path. oracle_files (gold_set) still from ground_truth.json/inline; commit unchanged.
- provenance: External (commit) first (unchanged); else NativeComposed when distinct backend identities >=2; else None. Auditable: backend names on the loaded task.
- CodeprobeTask::accepted_solution_files() -> Vec<BTreeSet> DEDUPED (BTreeSet<BTreeSet>) — shared by bridge.edit_locality_anchors + eval_run MetricInput. Fixes architect HIGH-1: identical backend file-sets dedup -> <2 distinct -> loud InsufficientAcceptedSolutions, never a degenerate floor==ceiling.
- Rewrite stale docs (architect MEDIUM-3): task.rs:28, provenance.rs:9, lib.rs:13, loader.rs:160.
FIXTURES (both trees: aoa-bench/tests/fixtures + aoa/tests/fixtures/codeprobe_tasks): native-consensus-001 -> remove ground_truth_ast.json, add divergence_report.json (decision shipped, 2 backends ast+treesitter). 
TESTS: provenance unit (2 distinct backends -> NativeComposed incl. identical-files case; 1 -> None; external -> External; quarantined report -> None); loader_test (backend identity populated, edit-locality dedup); cli.rs unaffected (native-consensus-001 stays NativeComposed).
STILL DEFERRED -> follow-up bead: null/all-zeros ground_truth_commit rejection (NOTE/aoa-2lw).
REVIEW (Phase-4, 3 agents: code-reviewer + rust-reviewer + architect): all APPROVE, 0 CRITICAL/HIGH blocking. Full workspace test + clippy green.
VERIFIED: External short-circuit precedence; NativeComposed fires for 2 distinct backends incl. identical-files (the fix); decision=='shipped' gate (quarantined/absent -> empty); serde #[serde(default)] cannot panic on untrusted JSON; dead backend_variant_files/ground_truth_ast fully removed (grep clean); 16MiB read_file cap (aoa-d6t.1) preserved on the new divergence_report read; native-consensus-001 stays NativeComposed for the RIGHT reason (2 backends in report); all cli.rs integration green.
FOLDED FIXES:
- architect MEDIUM-2 (silent shipped->None): dropped the !files.is_empty() filter from read_accepted_backends so an empty-file backend can't make AOA override codeprobe's shipped decision; emptiness now handled only in accepted_solution_files (edit-locality). +test empty_file_backend_counts_for_provenance_but_not_edit_locality.
- architect HIGH-1 (fictional pair_metrics sub-schema in fixture): corrected to codeprobe's real shape {backend_a,backend_b,precision,recall,f1} in both fixture trees.
- code-reviewer MEDIUM (missing edit-locality identical-files test): added identical_backend_solutions_are_insufficient_for_edit_locality (NativeComposed + InsufficientAcceptedSolutions(1)).
- architect MEDIUM-1 (consensus_files vs G_t split-brain): doc note added to read_accepted_backends.
NOT CHANGED (judgment): decision serde-default kept (conservative-safe: missing decision -> not shipped -> None, never fabricates provenance); path.exists TOCTOU + dedup allocation are LOW/cosmetic on a non-hot path.
DEFERRED null-SHA fix filed as aoa-d6t.9 (P2).
close_reason: DONE: Re-grounded NativeComposed provenance on codeprobe's REAL artifact divergence_report.json (schema consensus.v1), replacing a file-set-equality dedup that collapsed identical-file backends to None and a fictional ground_truth_<backend>.json source that codeprobe never emits. New AcceptedSolution{backend,files} carries identity; classify_provenance counts distinct backends; read_accepted_backends consumes codeprobe's decision=='shipped' (ZFC, no F1 re-derivation); accepted_solution_files() dedups+drops-empty for the edit-locality boundary (identical/empty backends -> loud InsufficientAcceptedSolutions, never degenerate floor==ceiling). Removed dead variant path. Phase-4 (3 reviewers, all APPROVE) folded: dropped files-empty filter to stop silent shipped->None demotion, fixed fictional pair_metrics fixture sub-schema, added identical-files + empty-backend tests. Key discovery surfaced to user (ground_truth_<backend>.json fiction) and approved re-direction. Deferred null/all-zeros-SHA External fix -> aoa-d6t.9. cargo test (full workspace) + clippy green. Merged on main at a4a4bd6404dfcf07d29798e2b9e442f9727b46c0.

title: Harden untrusted-input parsing bounds (shim + scip-graph DoS)
description: Phase-4 security review of wave-setA. Three crates parse untrusted local input with no resource bounds: (1) aoa-codeprobe-shim/src/parse.rs reads whole transcript via read_to_string (no size cap), grows span_index_by_tool_id HashMap one entry per unique tool_use_id, and grows warnings Vec one per malformed line — all unbounded. (2) aoa-scip-graph/src/scip.rs index_with_scip + best_effort.rs read whole files with no size cap; node/edge BTreeSets grow unbounded from a crafted SCIP index. (3) aoa-scip-graph/src/best_effort.rs collect_py_files uses path.is_dir() which FOLLOWS symlinks — a symlink to / recurses the whole filesystem. DoS-only (local, attacker-controlled files); fix before any networked/multi-tenant use.
acceptance_criteria: 1) File-size guard before read_to_string in shim + scip + best_effort (reject oversized). 2) Cap warnings count and span_index entries in shim. 3) collect_py_files uses metadata().is_symlink() check (or walkdir follow_links(false)) — symlink dirs not traversed; tested. 4) cargo test + clippy green.
notes:
eval_run.rs (aoa-2lw) is now a direct call path to parse_transcript_file + build_symbol_graph over an untrusted --codeprobe-run dir, so the shim/scip-graph DoS bounds in this bead now gate a user-facing CLI entry point. Also cap task-dir count in eval_run discover_tasks.
SCOPE EXTENSION (from aoa-2ce Phase-4): two more untrusted-input sites now on user-facing call paths. (1) crates/aoa/src/commands/r0b.rs reads each trial scoring.json via read_to_string with no size cap (same class as eval_run.rs (1)) — add a file-size guard. (2) crates/aoa-bench/src/loader.rs backend_variant_files (~line 226) iterates ground_truth_<backend>.json siblings via read_dir + entry.path() WITHOUT a file_type/symlink guard, so a symlinked ground_truth_*.json is followed and its bytes can leak into a JSON-parse error — mirror the DirEntry::file_type symlink guard discover_tasks already uses. r0b now reaches this via load_task over an untrusted --tasks dir.
PLAN: Add resource bounds to every untrusted-input parse path in wave-setA.
(1) shim/parse.rs: MAX_TRANSCRIPT_BYTES (64MiB) size guard in parse_transcript_file -> new ShimError::TooLarge; MAX_SPANS + MAX_WARNINGS caps in parse_transcript (single truncation warning, break loop) — defends amplification (tiny-line files) the size cap alone misses.
(2) scip-graph/scip.rs: MAX_SCIP_BYTES (128MiB) guard in index_with_scip -> new ScipGraphError::TooLarge.
(3) scip-graph/best_effort.rs: per-file MAX_PY_BYTES (8MiB) guard; collect_py_files swaps path.is_dir()/extension() for entry.file_type() so symlinked dirs are NOT traversed (mirrors discover_tasks).
(4) aoa bin: new commands/fsutil.rs read_to_string_capped(); use in eval_run scoring read + codeprobe DualScoring::load (covers r0b scoring.json); cap task-dir count in discover_tasks (MAX_TASK_DIRS).
(5) aoa-bench/loader.rs: file_type symlink guard in backend_variant_files (require is_file); size guard in read_file.
Tests co-located: oversized rejection per crate, warnings/spans caps, symlink-dir-not-traversed, symlinked variant skipped, task-dir cap. cargo test + clippy green.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect adversarial pass, findings folded):
- H1: replace metadata().len() with File::open + Read::take(max+1) — TOCTOU-safe and symlink-target-safe for the local-attacker threat model. read_to_string after take bounds actual bytes held.
- C1: best_effort oversized .py -> SKIP file + continue (bounded memory, no whole-repo Degraded demotion via build_symbol_graph index.rs:55); documented in doc comment (honest, not silent_fallback).
- M1: shim span cap is FAIL-LOUD (parse_transcript -> Result, ShimError::TooLarge) — never silently truncate a trace feeding R0 metrics. Warnings cap keeps a non-silent 'N further warnings suppressed' sentinel (diagnostics are lossy; metrics are not). Caps generous: MAX_SPANS=200k, MAX_WARNINGS=10k, well above legit runs, below pathological (64MiB/~50B ~ 1.3M ceiling).
- H2: per-crate read_to_string_capped mapping to each native error (ShimError/ScipGraphError/BenchError/anyhow). No new shared leaf crate — shim/scip-graph/bench share no low-level util crate, and different error types make this justified extension not true duplication.
- C2 (DEFERRED to follow-up bead): remaining unbounded JSON loads outside this bead's named sites — falsify.rs repos/build_meta/bias aggregate.json, falsify_build.rs manifest, eval.rs compare run, aoa-trace validate-trace, r0b canary manifest. Filed separately to keep this diff reviewable.
Cap values: transcript 64MiB, scip index 128MiB, py file 8MiB, ground_truth 16MiB, task-dir count 100k.
PROGRESS: implemented all 5 sites + co-located tests, full workspace test + clippy green.
- shim: read_capped (Read::take, 64MiB) + Limits-bounded parse_transcript (fail-loud MAX_SPANS=200k, warnings sentinel MAX_WARNINGS=10k); ShimError::TranscriptTooLarge/TooManySpans; 3 unit tests.
- scip-graph: bounded.rs read_capped (128MiB scip / 8MiB source); ScipGraphError::TooLarge; best_effort collect_py_files uses entry.file_type() (symlink dirs not traversed), oversized .py skipped+continue (no whole-repo demotion); symlink-traversal test + cap test.
- aoa-bench/loader: read_capped (16MiB) via Read::take; backend_variant_files requires is_file (symlink variant skipped); BenchError::TooLarge; 2 unit tests.
- aoa bin: commands/fsutil.rs read_to_string_capped (anyhow, 16MiB) used in eval_run scoring + codeprobe DualScoring::load (covers r0b); MAX_TASK_DIRS=100k cap in discover_tasks; cap test.
parse_transcript signature changed to Result; only caller is parse_transcript_file (internal). All metadata().len() avoided -> Read::take (TOCTOU/symlink-safe).
REVIEW (Phase-4, 3 agents: code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer):

title: R9c: construct-validity correlation report (gate before any metric gates a feature)
description: Before the reward-hacking gap or any of the four metrics may GATE a generative feature, produce a correlation report tying it to >=1 external outcome (post-merge revert rate, production incident count, or human review-acceptance). Absent a positive correlation, the metric stays advisory. aoa-gap already distinguishes advisory vs gating; this supplies the real correlation data.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A correlation-report artifact ties each gating-candidate metric to >=1 external outcome (post-merge revert rate, production incident count, or human review-acceptance) with sign + magnitude, reproducible from a documented data source (codeprobe run history or external outcome data). 2) aoa_gap::classify_metric returns Gating ONLY when supplied a CorrelationReport with >=1 positive external outcome (already implemented); this issue supplies the real data and wires the decision — absent/negative correlation -> Advisory (tested). 3) cargo test passes where code is touched; build green.
notes:
codeprobe's bias detectors + dual-verifier mining already cover part of construct validity (tautology/overshipping/no-independent-baseline). R9c composes: a metric stays advisory until a correlation report ties it to >=1 EXTERNAL outcome (revert rate / incident count / review-acceptance).
PLAN: Make R9c construct validity REAL. Feasibility finding: NO external-outcome dataset (revert/incident/review) exists in repo or codeprobe runs; calibrated is a bare bool; only the oracle pass/reward is per-trial and is circular for construct validity. So the scientifically correct deliverable = the reproducible correlation apparatus + the honest "data insufficient -> all metrics stay Advisory" determination. NOT a fabricated positive correlation. Approach: (1) evolve OutcomeCorrelation from bare positive:bool to carry sign+magnitude (signed coefficient:f64 + n:usize) [AC1]; (2) new correlation.rs with deterministic Spearman rank coeff over &[(f64,f64)] (ZFC deterministic-math); (3) encode each ExternalOutcome quality-direction (revert/incident: higher metric should REDUCE -> negative coeff confirms; review-acceptance: positive confirms); (4) replace bare-bool has_positive_correlation with threshold-gated confirming check (sign matches direction AND |coeff|>=min_magnitude AND n>=min_n), thresholds as explicit data; (5) classify_metric Gating only on a confirming correlation, else Advisory; (6) reproducible artifact via documented fixture + serde round-trip test; (7) insufficient-data test: each gating-candidate metric (4 locality + budget + reward-hacking gap) stays Advisory absent data. Files: aoa-gap/src/construct.rs, NEW correlation.rs, lib.rs, tests/criteria.rs (update criterion_6). Out of scope: repo-clone/git-revert archaeology to build a real corpus (file follow-up), falsify calibrated wiring, aoa-dhk.1 scale run.
PLAN-REVIEW (architect): folded in. CRITICAL: magnitude+n gates are FP-theater without a significance test -> add deterministic EXACT PERMUTATION p-value (cap n<=10, typed error beyond; no RNG/incomplete-beta; matches small-n repo-level calibration regime). HIGH: binary outcomes (revert/review) produce massive ties -> use TIE-CORRECTED Spearman (Pearson-on-average-ranks), NOT the d^2 shortcut. HIGH: AC1 needs a real reproducible ARTIFACT not just a prose conclusion -> commit tests/fixtures/construct_validity_report.json (serialized report, data_source named, n=0->Advisory for all candidates) + a test that regenerates it from the documented empty source. MEDIUM: metric orientation must be EXPLICIT (HigherIsBetter/LowerIsBetter) combined with outcome direction -> confirming sign derived, not baked into ExternalOutcome (revert raw-coeff sign was backwards under raw orientation). MEDIUM: DELETE has_positive_correlation, fold into one threshold predicate; rewrite stale docs in construct.rs+lib.rs. MEDIUM: known-answer UNIT tests for the statistic in correlation.rs (hand-computed rho, tie case, small-n-high-rho-not-significant). Extend criterion_6: below-magnitude/below-n/below-significance/wrong-direction all -> Advisory. Confirmed circular: oracle pass & ground_truth_commit cannot serve as the external outcome.
REVIEW (3 agents): code-reviewer APPROVE (statistic verified against independent Python reference: tie-corrected Spearman, exact n! permutation p-value, all 6 orientation x outcome combos correct, artifact-reproduction test non-tautological). security-reviewer: no CRITICAL/HIGH (MAX_EXACT_N cap enforced before factorial/permutation; no overflow; fixture is compile-time include_str, not runtime untrusted input). rust-reviewer: warning, MEDIUM only. FIXED: NaN/inf observations now rejected with typed CorrelationError::NonFiniteObservation (was silently producing NaN coeff + p=0.0 from the public spearman boundary); added 6th-combination test (LowerIsBetter x ReviewAcceptance); one-pass unzip; debug_assert documenting factorial precondition; dropped Eq (f64 payload). DECLINED (design taste, not bugs): dated provenance string is an intentional fixture-locked finding date; GATING_CANDIDATES tuple + flat MetricClassification are deliberate (clean flat artifact) - structs/flatten would be anti-slop churn. Final: 10 unit + 15 integration tests green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, full workspace green.
